response.justification: Bogdan is currently embedded in exactly the right environment—working on AI-agent infrastructure and agentic systems at re:cinq—which is a powerful signal of genuine hands-on experience with the domain. His distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (handling millions of requests/second) and operational background demonstrate depth. However, the absence of Python from his skill set is a material gap for a must-have requirement, and there's limited explicit evidence of agile methodology, incident response, or stakeholder management across functions. Interview warranted if Python proficiency can be quickly established or if the hiring team is willing to invest in ramp-up.
strengths: Currently working in an AI-native environment contributing to agentic infrastructure and MCP frameworks—baseline competency already validated
Demonstrated ownership mindset and pace: founder of GISCollective, multiple startups, and full operational responsibility across roles

Proven distributed systems expertise from Dunnhumby (microservices handling millions of requests/second) and strong architectural thinking evidenced by teaching, code katas, and software craftsmanship practices
concerns: Python is a must-have skill but is entirely absent from the resume despite broad multi-language experience and recent full-stack work
Limited explicit evidence of agile methodology experience, operational incident response, or on-call rotation history required for staff-level ownership

Career shows breadth-first approach (web, mobile, desktop, embedded, languages, domains) rather than depth-first specialization—may indicate difficulty going deep on high-throughput architecture over time
